context.setting: Watching TV with TV on Right

episode_text:
day=28; meal=cantaloupes, bananas, watermelon; setting=Watching TV with TV on Right; time_of_day=morning;
preferences: microwave_time=no microwave; occlusion_relevance=minimize right occlusion; robot_speed=medium; skewering_axis=parallel to major axis; web_interface_confirmation=yes; transfer_mode=inside mouth transfer; outside_mouth_distance=not applicable; convey_robot_ready_for_initiating_transfer=LED; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_feeding=button; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_drinking=button; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_wiping=button; convey_robot_ready_for_completing_transfer=LED; detect_user_completed_transfer_feeding=perception; detect_user_completed_transfer_drinking=button; detect_user_completed_transfer_wiping=button; retract_between_bites=no; bite_dipping_preference=do not dip; wait_before_autocontinue_seconds=1000 sec

microwave_time.user_tendencies: IF food is fruit or another cold-served item THEN no microwave BECAUSE repeated fruit meals, including the new right-TV fruit meal, show the user preserves the intended cold serving temperature. IF the meal is a warm savory item in a quiet personal setting THEN 2 min BECAUSE observed personal savory meals support moderate reheating as the baseline in non-social, non-distraction contexts. IF the meal is a warm savory item in a social context, including with a person on the left, right, or front, THEN 1 min or 2 min depending on the specific meal BECAUSE observed social savory meals support mild-to-moderate reheating rather than hot extremes. IF the meal is a warm savory item while watching TV on the left THEN 1 min or 2 min depending on the specific meal BECAUSE observed left-TV meals support mild-to-moderate reheating rather than a fixed value. IF the meal is a warm savory item while watching TV on the right THEN 3 min, but IF the meal is fruit or another cold-served item in that same right-TV context THEN no microwave BECAUSE the right-TV savory evidence favors hotter food while the new right-TV fruit meal confirms food serving temperature overrides context. IF the meal is a warm savory item while working on a laptop on the right THEN 2 min BECAUSE repeated right-laptop work meals support a moderate reheating preference in that context. IF the meal is a warm savory item while working on a laptop on the left THEN 2 min BECAUSE repeated left-laptop work meals support a stable moderate reheating preference there. IF the meal is a warm savory item while working on a laptop in front THEN 1 min or 3 min depending on the specific meal BECAUSE front-work meals have shown both shorter and longer reheating, indicating meal-specific variation rather than one fixed work-context value.

occlusion_relevance.user_tendencies: IF dining context is a quiet personal setting THEN do not consider occlusion BECAUSE observed personal meals reinforce that occlusion handling is usually unnecessary when no social partner or focal side/front activity is present. IF dining context includes a social partner or focal activity directly in front of the user THEN minimize front occlusion BECAUSE repeated front-social, front-TV, and front-laptop meals consistently show the user wants the robot to avoid blocking the front. IF dining context includes a social partner or focal activity to the right of the user THEN minimize right occlusion BECAUSE repeated right-oriented contexts, now including the new right-TV meal, consistently show the user wants the robot to avoid blocking that side. IF dining context includes a social partner on the left THEN minimize left occlusion BECAUSE repeated left-social meals consistently show the user wants the robot to avoid blocking a person on that side. IF dining context involves watching TV on the left THEN minimize left occlusion or do not consider occlusion depending on the specific meal BECAUSE the user has selected both options in left-TV meals. IF dining context involves working on a laptop on the left THEN do not consider occlusion BECAUSE repeated left-laptop work meals have not triggered left-side occlusion avoidance.

robot_speed.user_tendencies: IF dining context is a quiet personal setting THEN medium BECAUSE observed personal meals support a moderate pace when eating alone without competing social or visual demands. IF dining context involves a social partner to the right THEN slow BECAUSE the user selected slow in repeated right-social meals, indicating a stable cautious pace in that setting. IF dining context involves a social partner to the left THEN slow or medium depending on the specific meal BECAUSE left-social meals have shown both values, with caution remaining prominent. IF dining context involves a social partner in front THEN slow BECAUSE direct social engagement in front favors a more cautious pace. IF dining context involves watching TV on the right THEN slow or medium depending on the specific meal BECAUSE the earlier right-TV savory meal favored slow while the new right-TV fruit meal selected medium, suggesting this context does not have a single fixed speed and may vary with meal-specific comfort or urgency. IF dining context involves watching TV on the left THEN slow or fast depending on the specific meal BECAUSE left-TV meals have shown both efficiency-seeking and caution. IF dining context involves working on a laptop to the right THEN medium BECAUSE repeated right-laptop work meals support a stable medium-speed preference in that context. IF dining context involves working on a laptop on the left THEN medium BECAUSE repeated left-laptop work meals support a stable medium pace. IF dining context involves a valued activity directly in front of the user, such as TV or laptop work, THEN medium or fast depending on the specific meal BECAUSE front-oriented non-social contexts have shown both moderate and efficiency-seeking speeds.

skewering_axis.user_tendencies: IF food acquisition must remain compatible with limited mouth opening in general THEN parallel to major axis BECAUSE the user's physical profile supports narrower bites and this remains the dominant observed choice, including the new fruit meal. IF the food is a bite-acquisition-challenging savory item such as chicken, fish bites, nuggets, sandwiches, pizza pieces, or similar solid pieces THEN perpendicular to major axis or parallel to major axis depending on the specific meal BECAUSE the user has sometimes selected the higher-acquisition-success option for solid savory foods, but repeated meals still often favor parallel when limited mouth opening remains the stronger concern.

transfer_mode.user_tendencies: IF the user's physical constraints are relevant to the meal context THEN inside mouth transfer BECAUSE the user cannot lean forward due to limited trunk control, and observed selections consistently favor the mode that does not require leaning forward.

outside_mouth_distance.user_tendencies: IF transfer_mode is inside mouth transfer THEN not applicable BECAUSE this parameter only applies to outside mouth transfer. IF the user's physical constraints are considered for food delivery THEN not applicable BECAUSE the observed preference and physical profile support inside mouth transfer rather than outside mouth transfer.

convey_robot_ready_for_initiating_transf.user_tendencies: IF dining context is a quiet personal setting THEN speech + LED BECAUSE observed personal meals show the user prefers a more salient multimodal cue when alone. IF dining context involves working on a laptop in front or to the right THEN LED BECAUSE the user selected LED in repeated front- and right-laptop work contexts, consistent with a low-disruption visual cue during work. IF dining context involves working on a laptop on the left THEN LED or speech + LED depending on the specific meal BECAUSE the user has selected both in left-laptop work meals, with LED remaining a common low-disruption choice there. IF dining context involves a social partner to the left, right, or front THEN LED BECAUSE repeated social meals across side and front positions show a preference for visual-only signaling in social settings. IF dining context involves watching TV in front THEN LED BECAUSE the user selected LED in front-TV meals, aligning with unobtrusive visual signaling when attention is directed forward. IF dining context involves watching TV on the left THEN speech or LED depending on the specific meal BECAUSE the user has selected both cue modalities in that context. IF dining context involves watching TV on the right THEN LED or speech + LED depending on the specific meal BECAUSE the earlier right-TV meal used a more salient multimodal cue while the new right-TV meal used LED, indicating a right-TV preference for either unobtrusive visual signaling or stronger salience depending on the specific meal.

detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfe.user_tendencies: IF initiating feeding transfer in contexts where physical ability constraints matter and no stronger contextual pressure is observed THEN button BECAUSE the user can press buttons reliably, while limited mouth opening makes open-mouth interaction challenging. IF dining context is a quiet personal setting THEN button BECAUSE observed personal meals reinforce that the user prefers explicit manual control even when alone. IF dining context involves working on a laptop in front, on the left, or to the right THEN button BECAUSE the user selected button across observed work contexts. IF dining context involves a social partner on the left, right, or front THEN button BECAUSE repeated social meals reinforce that social settings do not override the preference for explicit manual signaling. IF dining context involves watching TV in front or on the right THEN button BECAUSE the user selected button in those TV contexts, including the new right-TV meal. IF dining context involves watching TV on the left THEN button or autocontinue depending on the specific meal BECAUSE the user has selected both options in that context. IF a context would require choosing between open mouth interaction and button input THEN button BECAUSE limited mouth opening makes open-mouth interaction less suitable for this user.

IF initiating drinking transfer in contexts where physical ability constraints matter and no stronger contextual pressure is observed THEN button BECAUSE the user can press buttons reliably, while limited mouth opening makes open-mouth interaction challenging. IF dining context is a quiet personal setting THEN button BECAUSE observed personal meals reinforce that the user prefers explicit manual control even when alone. IF dining context involves working on a laptop in front, on the left, or to the right THEN button BECAUSE the user selected button across observed work contexts. IF dining context involves a social partner on the left, right, or front THEN button BECAUSE repeated social meals support explicit manual signaling. IF dining context involves watching TV in front or on the right THEN button BECAUSE the user selected button in those TV contexts, including the new right-TV meal. IF dining context involves watching TV on the left THEN button or autocontinue depending on the specific meal BECAUSE the user has selected both options in that context. IF a context would require choosing between open mouth interaction and button input THEN button BECAUSE limited mouth opening makes open-mouth interaction less suitable for this user.

IF initiating mouth-wiping transfer in contexts where physical ability constraints matter and no stronger contextual pressure is observed THEN button BECAUSE the user can press buttons reliably, while limited mouth opening makes open-mouth interaction challenging. IF dining context is a quiet personal setting THEN button BECAUSE observed personal meals reinforce that the user prefers explicit manual control even when alone. IF dining context involves working on a laptop in front, on the left, or to the right THEN button BECAUSE the user selected button across observed work contexts. IF dining context involves a social partner on the left, right, or front THEN button BECAUSE repeated social meals support explicit manual signaling. IF dining context involves watching TV in front or on the right THEN button BECAUSE the user selected button in those TV contexts, including the new right-TV meal. IF dining context involves watching TV on the left THEN button or autocontinue depending on the specific meal BECAUSE the user has selected both options in that context. IF a context would require choosing between open mouth interaction and button input THEN button BECAUSE limited mouth opening makes open-mouth interaction less suitable for this user.

convey_robot_ready_for_completing_transf.user_tendencies: IF dining context is a quiet personal setting THEN speech + LED BECAUSE observed personal meals show the user prefers a more salient multimodal completion cue when alone. IF dining context involves working on a laptop in front or to the right THEN LED BECAUSE the user selected LED in repeated front- and right-laptop work contexts, consistent with a low-disruption visual cue during work. IF dining context involves working on a laptop on the left THEN LED or speech + LED depending on the specific meal BECAUSE the user has selected both in left-laptop work meals, with LED remaining a common low-disruption choice there. IF dining context involves a social partner to the left, right, or front THEN LED BECAUSE repeated social meals across side and front positions show a preference for visual-only completion cues in social settings. IF dining context involves watching TV in front THEN LED BECAUSE the user selected LED in front-TV meals. IF dining context involves watching TV on the left THEN speech or LED depending on the specific meal BECAUSE the user has selected both cue modalities in that context. IF dining context involves watching TV on the right THEN LED or speech + LED depending on the specific meal BECAUSE the earlier right-TV meal used a more salient multimodal cue while the new right-TV meal used LED, indicating a right-TV preference that varies between unobtrusive visual-only and stronger multimodal completion signaling.

detect_user_completed_transfer_feeding.user_tendencies: IF completing feeding transfer in contexts where physical ability constraints matter THEN perception BECAUSE the user selected the reliable perception-based option across observed contexts, including personal, work, social, and TV meals, while avoiding extra physical input.

detect_user_completed_transfer_drinking.user_tendencies: IF completing drinking transfer in contexts where physical ability constraints matter and no stronger contextual pressure is observed THEN button BECAUSE the user cannot reliably perform head gestures, making perception-based drinking completion less suitable. IF dining context is a quiet personal setting THEN button BECAUSE observed personal meals reinforce that the user prefers explicit completion signaling even when alone. IF dining context involves working on a laptop in front, on the left, or to the right THEN button BECAUSE the user selected button across observed work contexts. IF dining context involves a social partner on the left, right, or front THEN button BECAUSE repeated social meals support explicit completion signaling. IF dining context involves watching TV in front or on the right THEN button BECAUSE the user selected button in those TV contexts, including the new right-TV meal. IF dining context involves watching TV on the left THEN button or autocontinue depending on the specific meal BECAUSE the user has selected both options in that context. IF a context would require choosing between perception and button for drinking completion THEN button BECAUSE limited neck control makes head-gesture-based perception less suitable.

detect_user_completed_transfer_wiping.user_tendencies: IF completing mouth-wiping transfer in contexts where physical ability constraints matter and no stronger contextual pressure is observed THEN button BECAUSE the user cannot reliably perform head gestures, making perception-based wiping completion less suitable. IF dining context is a quiet personal setting THEN button BECAUSE observed personal meals reinforce that the user prefers explicit completion signaling even when alone. IF dining context involves working on a laptop in front, on the left, or to the right THEN button BECAUSE the user selected button across observed work contexts. IF dining context involves a social partner on the left, right, or front THEN button BECAUSE repeated social meals support explicit completion signaling. IF dining context involves watching TV in front or on the right THEN button BECAUSE the user selected button in those TV contexts, including the new right-TV meal. IF dining context involves watching TV on the left THEN button or autocontinue depending on the specific meal BECAUSE the user has selected both options in that context. IF a context would require choosing between perception and button for wiping completion THEN button BECAUSE limited neck control makes head-gesture-based perception less suitable.

retract_between_bites.user_tendencies: IF dining context is a quiet personal setting THEN no BECAUSE observed personal meals reinforce that the user prefers to skip extra retraction time when no social partner or valued visual target needs protection from obstruction. IF dining context includes a social partner or focal activity directly in front of the user THEN yes BECAUSE repeated front-oriented contexts, including front-social, front-TV, and front-laptop meals, show the user prefers retraction to reduce obstruction. IF dining context includes a social partner to the left or right THEN yes BECAUSE repeated side-social meals show that social presence favors reducing obstruction. IF dining context involves a focal activity to the right without a social partner THEN no BECAUSE the user repeatedly selected no retraction while working on a laptop to the right and the new right-TV meal also selected no, reinforcing that right-side non-social activities do not by themselves trigger retraction. IF dining context involves watching TV on the left THEN no BECAUSE the user has repeatedly selected no retraction in that left-TV context. IF dining context involves working on a laptop on the left THEN no BECAUSE repeated left-laptop work meals support skipping retraction in that context.

bite_dipping_preference.user_tendencies: IF food does not include a dip or sauce THEN do not dip BECAUSE dipping is not relevant and the user selected no dipping for non-dippable meals, including the new fruit meal. IF food includes a dip or sauce AND dining context is social THEN less or do not dip depending on the meal BECAUSE the user consistently avoids heavy dipping in social settings, reinforcing a reduced-mess preference. IF food includes a dip or sauce AND dining context involves a front-focused activity such as working on a laptop or watching TV in front THEN less BECAUSE the user repeatedly selected less dipping for sauce-containing meals in front-oriented contexts. IF food includes a dip or sauce AND dining context involves working on a laptop on the left THEN less BECAUSE repeated left-laptop meals with sauce support a stable reduced-dipping preference there. IF food includes a dip or sauce AND dining context involves watching TV on the right THEN less BECAUSE the observed right-TV meal with ketchup shows the user prefers reduced dipping in that context. IF the meal has sauce already on the food but no separate dipping action is desired, including in left-TV contexts, THEN do not dip BECAUSE the user selected no dipping for sauced savory meals in that context.

wait_before_autocontinue_seconds.user_tendencies: IF dining context is a quiet personal setting THEN 100 sec BECAUSE observed personal meals provide direct evidence that the user prefers a moderate wait time when alone, rather than the longest delay used in more distraction-heavy contexts. IF dining context involves working on a laptop in front, on the left, or to the right THEN 1000 sec BECAUSE the user selected the longest wait across observed work contexts, consistent with preserving ample time to intervene while engaged in a work task. IF dining context involves a social partner on the left, right, or front THEN 1000 sec BECAUSE repeated social meals across side and front positions show a preference for ample intervention time in social settings. IF dining context involves watching TV in front or on the right THEN 1000 sec BECAUSE the user selected the longest wait in those TV contexts, including the new right-TV meal. IF dining context involves watching TV on the left THEN 10 sec or 1000 sec depending on the specific meal BECAUSE observed left-TV meals vary between quick automatic progression and preserving ample intervention time.
